Builders Inc. the cost deficiency of ₱11,054.00 or to demand payment for
such amounts, as the case may be; and
c. Municipal General Services Officer, Property Inspector and Municipal
Engineer strictly comply with the preparation of Purchase Order,
Inspection and Acceptance Report and Programs of Works pursuant to
Section 74 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Annex A Guidelines for the
Implementation of Infrastructure Projects By Administration of GPPB.
d. Municipal General Services monitor and institute corrective measures on
the findings by the COA-TAS;
e. Municipal Engineer monitor and institute corrective measures on the
findings by the COA-TAS or in the alternative the Municipal Accountant
demand from the amount due to Las Minas De Oro Construction the
64
cost deficiency of ₱68,736.59 or to demand payment for such amounts, as
the case may be; and
f. Municipal Accountant and Engineer submit the approved Work
Suspension Order (WSO) and Work Resumption Order (WRO) to
further evaluate the grant of extension of contract time to FS Sugay
Construction and Supplies, otherwise the Municipal Accountant demand
the payment of liquidated damages of ₱166,598.88 to the said contractor.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) secure copy of disbursement vouchers and
liquidation reports to determine the status of the dormant account
totaling ₱1,379,941.57 for the implementation of the program/projects
and/or return of the remaining balance to their respective NGAs; and
(ii) cause the transfer of completed projects under the Trust Fund to the
corresponding asset account in the General Fund; and
b. Municipal Treasurer (i) return to DILG the remaining balance of
completed project for reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the
General Fund pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-13; and
(ii) prioritize the return to the Bureau of Treasury the amount of
₱2,773.53 representing remaining balance of PDAF for reversion to the
unappropriated surplus of the GF of the National Government in
compliance with the decision of the Supreme Court in Belgica and
Araullo.

We recommend that the:

BAC (i) submit satisfactory explanation on the observation noted above
and (ii) strictly adheres to the provisions of the 2016 RIRR of RA
No. 9184 for the above-mentioned infrastructure projects and
procurement of goods; and
Municipal Accountant, Engineer and BAC submit the necessary
documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) require the Samahang
Pangkabuhayan ng mga Taga Dulangan to submit the lacking documents and
(ii) henceforth, comply with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and
COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 in granting of financial
assistance to NGOs/POs.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman furnish the Office of the Auditor
with the copy of executed contracts, PO and its pertinent supporting documents
within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer allocate 5% of the
annual budget pursuant to Section 2.3 of Philippine Commission of Women (PCW),
National Economic Development Authority (NEDA) and Department of Budget and
Management (DBM) Joint Circular No. 2012-01. Moreover, the GAD Focal Person
ensure the implementation of PPAs to achieve the objectives of GAD.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator ensure that the 20% DF is appropriated
and disbursed only for the priority projects, programs and activities
mentioned in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017;
b. Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant review and analyze the
unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for
reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322
of RA No. 7160; and
c. Municipal Engineer periodically provide the status of projects
undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer
and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of
balances of continuing appropriations, and submit a list of all completed
projects to support the reversion of such unexpended balance to the
unappropriated surplus.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Team and instruct them to conduct complete inventory of all of its property, plant and equipment. Proper planning should be considered such as preparation of plan specifying the date and timeframe of physical inventory, and the composition of the team who will conduct the physical inventory. Previous inventory reports and the list of PPEs per type/category from the accounting office must be available for ready reference and ARE must be available for fast tracing of the property and equipment. During the conduct of inventory, the Committee should list all PPEs found on hand. Property tagging must be made. Prepare the inventory report in accordance with MNGAS. Semi-expendable items should be identified and segregated from the inventory report of PPEs and must be prepared in a separate report. After the physical inventory-taking, the inventory committee shall reconcile the results of the count with property and accounting records; b. Municipal Accountant and MGSO maintain ledger and stock or property cards, respectively, and undertake the reconciliation of the result of the physical count of PPE with their respective records for fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements. Moreover, the MGSO start maintaining property records based on the deliveries of supplies or property for CY 2016 to date on the basis of the AIR and other supporting documents; c. Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee that will conduct inventory of the local roads under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System and submit the same to this office not later than January 31 of each year; and d. Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer and Assessor to exert their best efforts to gather the necessary documents to complete the requirements for the processing of the TCT of five parcels of land in favor of the Municipality of Puerto Galera.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued the Executive Order No. 18-2017 creating the Inventory Team of the Municipality of Puerto Galera, Oriental Mindoro and defining their Functions and Responsibilities. A List of unserviceable Properties was provided to the Designated Property Officer for each office. The Management availed services for the survey of various parcels of land subject for titling. The PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards were prepared by the Accounting Office and the Designated Property Officer for the year 2017. The Management started maintaining property records based on the deliveries of supplies or property for CY 2016 to date on the basis of the AIR and other supporting documents.
observations.reason: The absence of turnover of documents and properties from the previous administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. GSO-Designate prepare complete list of insurable property to facilitate the processing of insurance with GSIS so that the properties of the Municipality can be adequately and reliably protected against any damage to or loss; and b. Municipal Budget Officer include in the ensuing budget the appropriation for the premiums of insurance of all the insurable properties with GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Six motors and Municipal Building were covered with GSIS insurance policy. The Budget Officer will incorporate in the Annual Budget 2018, the "Property Insurance Fund."

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant analyze and verify the accuracy of the above-mentioned adjusting journal entries made in 2014 and the Land Improvement account and prepare a subsidiary record for each component of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card; b. Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee that will conduct inventory of the local roads under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare the Report of Local Road Network (RLRN) and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC); and c. Municipal Engineer-Designate provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer-Designate with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued the Executive Order No. 18-2017 creating the Inventory Team of the Municipality of Puerto Galera, Oriental Mindoro and defining Their Functions and Responsibilities. The Accountant will (i) maximize all the efforts to revisit these accounts since these were recorded more than 5 years ago. The accounts were still to be located; (ii) will prepare a subsidiary record for each component of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC); and (iii) will start preparing the subsidiary ledgers and LRNLC for the road components for the year 2017, then will gradually prepare those from the previous years. The Designated Property Officer will prepare the Report of Local Road Network (RLRN) and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC). Municipal Engineer Designate will (i) provide the Accountant and the Designated Property Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and (ii) assist the Accountant and the Designated Property Officer-Designate in determining the components of the road networks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant assign one staff that will prepare the bank reconciliation statements in order to update the bank reconciliation statements and prepare the necessary adjustment to affected records; and (ii) prepare the BRS every end of each month for all bank accounts and submits the same within the 10 days from the receipt of Bank Statement; b. Management immediately close the account dormant account with DBP to avoid possible bank charges and surcharges; and c. Municipal Treasurer (i) reconcile the cash book balance with the ledger of the Accounting Office; (ii) prepare cash book for Trust Fund – EUF and MDK Program; and (iii) submit the bank statement of DBP to verify the balance as of December 31, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted BRS of General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF) for the period of January to December 2017. On July 12, 2017, Resolution no. 102-07-2017 was issued by the Sangguniang Bayan authorizing the Mayor and the Treasurer for the reactivation and closing of the dormant account with account no. 0605-012998-080 at DBP. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer already reconciled their records.
observations.reason: The problem in Cash deficiencies accumulated from the past six years that’s why even though the Municipality already exceeded its estimated income in 2016, the Municipality’ s cash flow efficiency was affected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Finance Committee strictly observe the provisions of Section 344 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and undertake the following: (i) Adopt measures to effectively control and monitor the disbursement of funds and ensure that funds are used exclusively for the specific purpose/s for which they have been appropriated pursuant to Sections 305(a) and 336 of RA 7160; (ii) Increase its income such as intensifying tax collection efficiency and investing in revenue-generating projects or activities; and (iii) Set aside fund to cover up the cash deficiencies; and b. Strong support of the Municipal Officials concerned for a continuing tight control of all expenditures is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The Finance Committee agrees about the adoption of measures to effectively control disbursement, increase its income, and other necessary financial measures as recommended by COA. The members of the finance committee are always coordinating with each other regarding the status of spending capacity of the Municipality whenever there were projects and expenditures that were planned to be pursued. The committee always gives recommendation to the Mayor to effectively control the disbursements.
observations.reason: The problem in Cash deficiencies accumulated from the past six years that’s why even though the Municipality already exceeded its estimated income in 2016, the Municipality’ s cash flow efficiency was affected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Install control over the issuance of EUF Tickets and AF 51 and its remittance by designating an employee independent from Municipal Treasurer’s Office that will randomly check its issuance and its remittance to the Liquidating Officer; and b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) Stop issuing accountable forms to erring accountable officers in order to avoid the practice of keeping collections in their hands for a long period of time; and (ii) Refrain from issuing EUF Tickets to non-bonded employees pursuant to Section 101 of PD 1445, Section 305(f) of RA 7160, Section 50, Chapter 9, Subtitle B, Book V, EO No 292 and Bureau of Treasury, Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 5, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Accounting Office to conduct cash count as of July 6, 2017 on July 7, 2017. The cash counted tied with the balance per book as of July 6, 2017. The Accountant and the Treasurer made a reconciliation of the cash in vault between balance accounting books and balance per cashbook. On the initial investigation it was noted that in 2015 and 2016, the deposit per accounting books were understated by ₱168,250.00 and ₱572,700.00, respectively. The Accountant already assigned staff to further investigate the balances. The Treasurer scheduled daily remittance of collection at 3pm. He assigned one person to monitor the remittance. If anyone could not remit on the said time of the day he will be called at his office for disciplinary action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. MDRRMO (i) prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to reflect the allocation for QRF in lump sum and disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness prepared with details as to projects and activities to be funded, as well as the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; (ii) ensure that all the proposed programs/projects for disaster be implemented during the year to ascertain readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity; and (iii) and in case there is change in the previously proposed projects, see to it that the disaster council is guided by DILG, DBM and National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 wherein the unexpended balance of disaster fund may be released and utilized for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised AIP as approved by the local Sanggunian and the LCE upon recommendation in the council and in accordance with the approved disaster plan; b. Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in consonance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; c. BAC (i) explain the deficiencies noted above; and (ii) strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement of vehicles; d. Municipal Accountant and BAC submit the supporting documents to substantiate the transactions; and e. Municipal Accountant (i) draw Journal Entry Voucher for the erroneous recording of unexpended balance; and (ii) maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfer of unutilized calamity fund to trust fund by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMFIP will be based on four thematic areas using prescribed format. This will also be integrated in the AIP and Annual Budget for 2018. Full coordination of the concerned officials regarding the incurred expenses for the activities of the MDRRMO was implemented. The unexpended balances as of December 31, 2017 will be incorporated in the MDRRMP and AIP for the year 2018. The Subsidiary Ledger will be maintained for the Municipality’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund as of 2017. The Accountant is preparing the monthly and quarterly utilization of the Calamity Fund. Submission will be made to COA as soon as possible. The BAC Secretariat submitted documents to the Accounting Office last August 23, 2017. The Municipal Accountant will draw Journal Entry Voucher for the erroneous recording of unexpended balance; and maintain Subsidiary Ledger for transfer of unutilized calamity fund to trust fund by year of transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and officials concerned: a. Establish an SWM facility that can be used for a long-term period instead of putting up a temporary one and eventually closing it due to non-compliance or other issue and obtain an ECC to comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9003 and PD No. 1586 for the establishment of solid waste management facility; b. Strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the operation of a dumpsite/sanitary landfill instead of the existing open dumpsite; c. Ensure that the constituents in the remaining 11 barangays illegally dumping their waste elsewhere were informed of proper waste disposal; and d. Call the attention of the junkshop owner in Barangay. Villaflor located just along the national highway to properly file the wastes and immediately dispose or recycle them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will exert all efforts for the expeditious issuance of ECC and other related documents needed for the Proposed Sanitary Landfill extension site in Villaflor, Puerto Galera. The Administration had proposed an area in Sitio Pagturian, Villaflor to serve as a sanitary landfill extension of the municipality. A public consultation in Villaflor was conducted for the use of an area in Sitio Pagturian as a proposed disposal facility. The MENRO has installed clear signage and a material recovery facility within said area. The scattered recyclables were also arranged in a designated MRF. The LGU with the supervision of MENRO will work overtime and maintain the stand for the development of a clean and green municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal MSWMB in assistance with other department head prioritize the revision of the SWMP in accordance with Section 17 of R.A No. 9003 for submission and approval by the NSWMC.
observations.managementAction: The Management is still in the process of collating documents and other requirements prescribed by Section 17 of RA No. 9003. The MENRO updated the waste analysis component of SWM Plan and identify the possible site for their Sanitary Landfill. The SWM Plan is still under review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. SB Members concerned provide explanation relative to their attendance in the aforementioned events and provide sufficient proof to their actual attendance in the events organized by the PCL and provide sufficient proof to their actual attendance in the events; b. Municipal Accountant (i) remind the employees concerned to liquidate their CAs within the prescribed period provided in Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; (ii) closely monitor the liquidation of CAs; (iii) immediately issue demand letter to officials and employees concerned with pending CAs; and ensure that all claims must be supported with complete and valid documentation, in case of the liquidation of CAs, scrutinize the Liquidation Reports and see to it that it was supported with valid documents required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the preparation of a particular Journal Entry Voucher taking up the liquidation; c. Municipal Treasurer submit all the liquidation reports for the CAs granted to officers and employees totaling ₱43,124.00 to ascertain the full liquidation of cash advances; and d. Municipal officials and employees concerned with CA for travel immediately liquidate their CAs upon arrival from each travel and attach to the liquidation report the copies of travel orders to ascertain the approved travel; e. Accountable officers liquidate the remaining outstanding cash advances within the prescribed regulatory period set by COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Explanation and proof of attendance was submitted. The Accountant will instruct a staff to update the report on unliquidated cash advances (RUCA) as frequently as possible to ensure proper monitoring of those with pending CAs. Demand letters will eventually be issued. The office had already submitted the Statement of Unliquidated Cash Advance for the 1st and 2nd quarter of 2017 last August 4, 2017. The Accountant will place a control to stop granting of another cash advance despite of non-liquidation of previous cash advance. The Accountant have already talked with the staff regarding the checking of the records from ledgers if there were pending liquidations from the concerned officials before coding journal entries to the vouchers. The liquidation reports were submitted to COA last March 31, 2017. The proof of receipt of the accommodation of Mayor Rockey D. Ilagan was submitted. The Accountant will continue to remind the concerned officials regarding their lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials and Employees concerned submit the necessary lacking documents for the payment of travelling expenses and ensure the compliance with the provisions set forth in Section 4 of EO No. 298 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant issued the Memorandum on August 17, 2017 to remind all the employees and officials regarding the rules on travel expenses as stated in EO 298. The concerned officials will maximize all efforts to submit the lacking documents to the Accountant. The Accountant will eventually submit such to COA. The proof of receipt of the accommodation of Mayor Rockey D. Ilagan was submitted. The Accountant will continue to remind the concerned officials regarding their lacking documents.
observations.reason: Verification of the submitted documents disclosed that the concerned officials and employees still failed to submit the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Officials and Employees concerned observe strictly the austerity measures set by DILG and minimize attendance to "Lakbay-Aral", instead appropriate funds to programs/projects/activities that will bring immediate socio-economic benefits among its local constituents; b. Municipal Budget Officer explain the excess charging of the expenses/disbursement against the appropriated amount for the Employees’ Day and the Lakbay-Aral for the BHW, BNS among others; c. Municipal Accountant submit the lacking documents to facilitate audit as to legality and propriety of the disbursement, otherwise the transaction would be considered irregular and refund of the full amount shall be made in full immediately; and d. BAC strictly adhere to the provision of Revised IRR of RA 9184 for the procurement of services and explain the deficiencies noted above.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will observe the austerity measures set by DILG. The BAC Secretariat submitted documents to the Accounting Office last August 23, 2017. The Accountant will maximize all the efforts to locate all the necessary documents from the previous officials / in-charge for submission to COA.
observations.reason: Verification of the submitted documents disclosed that the concerned officials and employees still failed to submit the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman explain the noted observation and submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the documents. However, verification of such disclosed that some of the required documents were still not submitted. Hence, the Audit Team issued Notice of Suspension No. 18-001-101-Puerto Galera (16) dated January 29, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman explain the noted observation and submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the documents. However, verification disclosed that some of the required documents were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor and Officials observe strictly the issuance of checks to financial assistance clients in accordance with the provisions set in Section VI B.3.1 of DSWD AO. No. 5 series of 2011; b. Municipal Accountant submit the necessary supporting documents; c. Local Chief Executive formulate clear-cut policies and well-defined guidelines for the granting of all types of donations/financial assistance, which shall provide among others, the following: (i) a clear definition of the qualified beneficiaries and what kind of assistance or donation is chargeable against a specific fund; (ii) the criteria in determining the amount of donation to be given in every case and the designated person/s that shall be responsible to determine the same; and (iii) complete list of documents to be required for each type of donation/assistance. Moreover, apportionment of funds to more beneficial programs and projects should be carefully considered in order to achieve the desired betterment of the whole constituency; and d. Municipal Accountant submit the missing vouchers and their complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO will maximize all effort in explaining to the clients the need to complete the documents required before claiming their assistance. All clients submitting documents in photocopies will be required to be certified true copies by issuing/concerned agencies. However, the LGU has no Crisis Intervention Unit and it has only 2 registered social workers. The MSWDO is strictly implementing the submission of supporting documents attached to respective vouchers for any assistance requested by any client started July 2017. The Accountant with the help of the Mayor’s Office has already communicated with the concerned agencies and persons with regards to the liquidation of the various funds granted to them for the year 2017. The Accountant will scrutinize the purpose of each of the financial assistance to be given to barangays or private individuals/organizations to abide with Section 335 of RA No. 7160. The Accountant will require all the grantees to submit liquidation reports to justify the significance of these expenditures as to the betterment of the entire populace.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Budget Officer (i) provide appropriation for salaries of the contractual personnel in the Municipality’s annual budget; (ii) strictly apply AO No. 47 and Section 322 relative to the use of savings; and (iii) ensure that funds shall be available exclusively for the specific purpose for which they have been appropriated pursuant to Section 336 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer will properly include the appropriations of salaries for contractual personnel of the Municipality in ensuing budget for 2018 and will strictly abide the guidelines mentioned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit copy of documents showing the written conformance from the Solicitor General or the Government Corporate Counsel and written concurrence of the Commission on Audit to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management terminated the contract of the current acting legal consultant effective July 1, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer and Waterworks System officials adopt austerity measures by minimizing the variable operating cost of the PGWS, and adopt a well-articulated policy for increased waterworks fee collection; b. Municipal officials concerned consider the options in Section 3.4 of the Local Budget Circular No. 100 wherein the personnel in the Operation of PGWS be transferred to the Plantilla of Personnel under the General Fund due to continuous incurrence of losses; and c. Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate financial statements for the LEE so that the Management can assess and consider the viability of the Waterworks System being an economic enterprise and for them to make well-informed decisions for its sustained financial viability over the long term while delivering goods and services to the general public.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a memorandum detaining various employees from the waterworks to the Treasurer’s Office to undergo supervision of the Treasurer when it comes to billing and collection of waterworks system. From an average collection of ₱578,000.00 from January to May 2017, the collection increased to ₱750,000.00 starting June 2017. The Mayor already requested for a resolution to the Sangguniang Bayan to lift the TEMPORARY SUSPENSION of the implementation of the increase of the Rates of Revenue Code of 2010 with regard to Article I-Charges for the Use of Waterworks System. The Accountant already ordered the staff handling the General Fund Accountant to prepare subsidiary ledger for Waterworks and for Market and Slaughter. The Accountant already ordered the staff to prepare the subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant submit the Liquidation Report for the grant of CA for the municipal share and financial assistance in the SY 2016-2017 provincial meet totaling ₱250,000.00 and comply with COA Circular No. 2012-001 or the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribing the documents needed in the grant and liquidation of CAs; b. Municipal Accountant refrain from granting CA to Municipal Treasurer for the payment of food, uniform and other expenses in the school meet if it is more practical or possible to make payment by checks; c. BAC Chairman, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer explain the above noted observations regarding the procurement and implementation of the project; and d. Municipal Accountant and BAC submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will observe the austerity measures. The BAC Secretariat submitted documents. The Accountant will not authorize granting of CA to the Treasurer for the payment of food, uniform and other expenses in the school meet if it is more practical or possible to make payment by checks. The BAC Secretariat submitted documents. The Accountant will maximize all the efforts to locate all the necessary documents from the previous officials / in-charge for submission to COA.
observations.reason: Verification of the submitted documents disclosed, however the BAC failed to submit all the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. BAC Chairman explain the procurement of rice from supplier other than NFA without securing waiver from the NFA; b. Sanggunian Bayan member submit justification for rice amounting to ₱41,800.00; and c. Henceforth, Management resort to NFA as primary supplier or source of rice in all its procurements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality secured waiver from NFA last August 8, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health Officer to maximize the utilization of the PFPR as required under the PhilHealth Circular No. 010 series of 2012 for the promotion of quality health care services; and b. Municipal Accountant prepare the quarterly PFP Disposition and Allocation Form and submit it to the Office of the Auditor for review in accordance with Section XII of PhilHealth Circular No. 015 series of 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality utilized the PFPR by purchasing one unit of inverter air-conditioner on June 2017. The Accountant prepared and submitted the PFPR Disposition and Allocation Form as of December 31, 2014, 2015 and 2016 and as of June 30, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer’s Office employee in-charge with the preparation of Report of Delinquent Market Stallholders provide the Municipal Accountant with amount due and penalties for unpaid market fees to facilitate timely recording of accrued rental income; and b. Municipal Accountant adopt the accrual basis of recording the stall fees pursuant to the Conceptual Framework for General Purpose Financial Reporting and follow PPSAS No. 13.
observations.managementAction: The employee in-charge in the preparation of the Report of Delinquent Market Stallholders will provide the Municipal Accountant with amount due and penalties for unpaid market fees to facilitate timely recording of accrued rental income. The Accountant will prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the unpaid market fees and its corresponding penalties for fairness of the balances in the financial statements of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant exhaust all the possible remedies provided under the existing rules and regulations to enforce collections of receivables outstanding as of December 31, 2016. If there are no possible remedies, the Municipal Mayor may file a request for write-off supported with the documents as itemized in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: According to the previous Designated Accountant, the collectability of the receivables from Rita P. Castillo and Leonides Balitaan, who died last September 25, 2007 and December 1, 2011, respectively, was remote since they were not able to receive any terminal benefits from the Municipality because these were all used to pay for their medication. Furthermore, receivables from constituents of Puerto Galera from a KKK program was already in the books for more than 20 years. Based from the response, the Municipal Accountant will consider writing off these accounts by complying all the necessary documents as itemized in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to the Commission on Audit. The Accountant already assigned three staff to accomplish the aging document needed. This will be submitted to the Mayor’s Office then a request letter for write off will be accomplished and submitted to COA as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the monthly monitoring Report of the Awarded Infra PPAs and List of Ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and b. Management pursue the timely submission of the reports within the prescribed period to avoid the automatic suspension of the payment of salary and other emoluments, and the imposition of administrative disciplinary action upon the defaulting personnel concerned.
observations.managementAction: The Engineer already assigned a staff to prepare the Monthly Monitoring Report of the Awarded Infra Projects as well as List of Ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year for the period of January to December 2017. The Municipal Engineering Office submitted the Monthly Monitoring Report of the Awarded Infra PPAs and List of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year for the period of January to December 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management abide with the rules set forth under Item 2.2.7 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to preserve public interest.
observations.managementAction: Management removed the name of the former Municipal Mayor on the landmarks, ambulance and manlift truck.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant -Designate and Treasurer agreed to effect the adjustments of identified reconciling items and draw Journal Entry Voucher to correct the balance per book of the depository accounts of the Municipality. Further, the municipal officials concerned shall seek assistance from the Executive and Legislative Departments for the undetermined/unexplained amount of ₱1,437,538.12 for appropriate action/s.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statement for General Fund and Special Education Fund was prepared by the Municipal Accountant for the period of January to December 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee (LFC) observe strict compliance with the PS limitation as mandated by Section 325(a) of RA 7160 in the preparation of annual/supplemental budgets. The Local Chief Executive should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are complied with.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has closely monitored disbursements under the personal services during the year. The PIB are still not refunded by the concerned municipal officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials concerned refrain from incurring such expenses and observe strict compliance with the provisions stated in the Appropriation Ordinance and the Municipal Accountant – Designate review the disbursement vouchers to determine completeness of supporting documents in compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations relative to travel.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is now strictly observing the rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the municipal officials and employees to comply with the findings of the Provincial Finance Committee on PS limitation and ensure that the appropriation and expenditures for Personal Services are within the 45% PS limitation as mandated by Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and DBM LBC 98 dated October 14, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had closely monitored disbursements under the personal services during the year. The additional benefits are still not refunded by the concerned municipal officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and other agencies concerned provide necessary training for the SWM staff to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and to minimize the risk of hazards to the SWM personnel and the public. We also recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board observe the standards and requirements for the collection, transportation and handling of solid wastes required by the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Management trained the staff of garbage collection team on the proper use of personal equipment such as gloves, mask and boots. The MENRO conducted regular education campaign on the waste conversion to various barangays and schools. The SWM staff attended necessary trainings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials concerned encourage the barangay to establish materials recovery facility that will receive the mixed wastes for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Moreover, we suggested that the Municipal’s SWM Board strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of RA No. 9003 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: Management started conducting Information Education Campaign in different sectors and monitored strictly the establishment of Material Recovery Facility in various barangays. The Municipality implemented centralized collections and no segregation no collection policy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of the Annual Budget, the Municipal Budget Officer correctly classify into Environment/Sanitary Services account the appropriation for the wages of SWM staff. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant record the cost of those expenses incurred in connection with the government operations/projects in their appropriate expense accounts to avoid misclassification of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is now recording the salary of SWM staff under Environment/Sanitary Services account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials and employees concerned to liquidate the unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2014; Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to closely monitor the liquidation of cash advances as soon as the settlement falls due by issuing demand letters to officials and employees concerned; and Municipal Accountant reclassify the cash advance of the deceased, Ms. Rita Castillo for ₱83,895.00 and the prior years’ cash advances without supporting documents totaling ₱47,000.00 to Other Receivables with disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The cash advances amounting to ₱388,037.00 was already liquidated. The cash advance of the deceased, Ms. Rita Castillo amounting to ₱83,895.00 and the prior years’ cash advance without supporting documents totaling ₱47,000.00 to Other Receivables were duly transferred with disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer effect the adjustments of identified reconciling items and draw Journal Entry Voucher to correct the balance per book of the depository accounts of the Municipality. Furthermore, make representation with the bank officials for the copies of bank debit/credit memo, analyze and verify the propriety and legality of the same and thereafter, make proper adjustments in the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statement for General Fund and SEF were prepared by the Municipal Accountant for the period of January to December 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare updated Bank Reconciliation Statements, reconcile and adjust the differences and thereafter submit the same to COA for audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: No Bank Reconciliation prepared for DBP account. The Municipality transferred the balance and closed the UCPB accounts in July 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board prepare an implementation and financial plan for the SEF budget which are in lump sum appropriation by indicating therein the proper classification of expenses to be incurred.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board is now preparing financial plan/annual budget indicating therein the proper classification of expenses to be incurred.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw journal entry vouchers to reclassify the transfer of the agency’s unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Liability-DRRM account and to record the transfer of the procured disaster and rescue equipment in the Trust to General Fund for the fair presentation of the PPE account in the financial statements; and observe the proper accounting procedure in recording the disbursements out of unexpended Quick Response and Mitigation Funds in the Trust Fund book of accounts by recording all expenses and assets for the expenditures incurred relative to DRRMF. Then at the end of the year, the expenses are credited and Trust Liability–DRRMF is being debited and the procured equipment/constructed structures should be transferred to the General Fund book.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is now transferring the unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Liability-DRRM M account to record the transfer of the procured disaster and rescue equipment in the Trust to General Fund for the fair presentation of the PPE account in the financial statements. Then at the end of the year, the expenses are credited and Trust Liability–DRRMF is being debited and the procured equipment/constructed structures will be transferred to the General Fund book. Also observe the proper accounting procedure in recording the disbursements out of unexpended Quick Response and Mitigation Funds in the Trust Fund book of accounts by recording all expenses and assets for the expenditures incurred relative DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor submit to COA complete justification as to the necessity of hiring van during his official travels within and outside the province for further assessment of its necessity.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Team was informed that justification as to the necessity of hiring van during his official travels within and outside the province for CY 2014 was submitted to the previous Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality ensure that all expenditures out of the EUF collections are within the authorized objects of expenditures as duly approved by the Sangguniang Bayan per recommendation by the Coastal Resource Management Board.
observations.managementAction: The municipal officials concerned exerted efforts to limit the charging of expenses against EUF within the authorized objects of expenditures. Provision for inclusion of operating and administrative costs, which are deemed necessary, in the Ordinance relative to EUF collections and utilization is still under study. No refund of overtime was being made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Coastal Resource Management Board include and recognize allowance for administrative and operating costs in its priority activities and fund appropriation from the Environmental Users’ Fee collections, subject to approval by the Sangguniang Bayan, pursuant to Section 7 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Puerto Galera Environmental Users’ Fee System.
observations.managementAction: The municipal officials concerned exerted efforts to limit the charging of expenses against EUF within the authorized objects of expenditures. No refund of overtime was being made.

observations.recommendation:
2.5. We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Team and instruct them to conduct
complete inventory of all of its property, plant and equipment. Proper
planning should be considered such as preparation of plan specifying the
date and timeframe of physical inventory, and the composition of the team
who will conduct the physical inventory. Previous inventory reports and the
list of PPEs per type/category from the accounting office must be available
for ready reference and ARE must be available for fast tracing of the
property and equipment. During the conduct of inventory, the Committee
should list all PPEs found on hand. Property tagging must be made. Prepare
the inventory report in accordance with MNGAS. Semi-expendable items
should be identified and segregated from the inventory report of PPEs and
must be prepared in a separate report. After the physical inventory-taking,
the inventory committee shall reconcile the results of the count with property
and accounting records;
b. Municipal Accountant and MGSO maintain ledger and stock or property
cards, respectively, and undertake the reconciliation of the result of the
physical count of PPE with their respective records for fair presentation of
the PPE accounts in the financial statements. Moreover, the MGSO start
maintaining property records based on the deliveries of supplies or property
for CY 2016 to date on the basis of the AIR and other supporting documents;
c. Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO-Designate and other officers concerned
to undertake the necessary actions regarding the disposal of its unserviceable
property, plant and equipment in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445
and Section 194 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property
Management in the Local Governments. Moreover, assess the utility of the
fully depreciated assets and consider disposing, in accordance with relevant
regulations, those of no use to prevent its further deterioration; and
d. Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer and Assessor to exert
their best efforts to gather the necessary documents to complete the
requirements for the processing of the TCT of five parcels of land in favor of
the Municipality of Puerto Galera. Coordination with the proper agency such
as Register of Deeds shall be made to facilitate the titling and/or transfer of
certificate of title in favor of the Municipality.

2.9 We further recommend that the Designated GSO submit the copy of OCT Nos.
P-5031, P-7291, P-8103 and P-7278 under the name of the Municipality for further
verification.

3.8. We recommended that the:
a. GSO-Designate prepare complete list of insurable property to facilitate
the processing of insurance with GSIS so that the properties of the
Municipality can be adequately and reliably protected against any
damage to or loss; and
b. Municipal Budget Officer include in the ensuing budget the
appropriation for the premiums of insurance of all the insurable
properties with GSIS.

4.9. We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant analyze and verify the accuracy of the above-
mentioned adjusting journal entries made in 2014 and the Land
Improvement account and prepare a subsidiary record for each component
of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card;
b. Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee that will conduct inventory
of the local roads under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare the
Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System and submit the
same to this office not later than January 31 of each year;
c. Property Officer-Designate prepare the Report of Local Road Network
(RLRN) and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a
complete Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC); and
d. Municipal Engineer-Designate provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Property Officer-Designate with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

5.9. We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) assign one staff that will prepare the bank
reconciliation statements in order to update the bank reconciliation
statements and prepare the necessary adjustment to affected records; and (ii)
prepare the BRS every end of each month for all bank accounts and submits
the same within the 10 days from the receipt of Bank Statement;
b. Management immediately close the account dormant account with DBP to
avoid possible bank charges and surcharges; and
c. Municipal Treasurer (i) reconcile the cash book balance with the ledger of
the Accounting Office; (ii) prepare cash book for Trust Fund – EUF and
MDK Program; (iii) submit the bank statement of DBP to verify the balance
as of December 31, 2016.

6.10. We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Finance Committee strictly observe the provisions of Section 344
of the Local Government Code of 1991 and undertake the following (i) Adopt
measures to effectively control and monitor the disbursement of funds and
ensure that funds are used exclusively for the specific purpose/s for which
they have been appropriated pursuant to Sections 305(a) and 336 of RA
7160; (ii) Increase its income such as intensifying tax collection efficiency and
investing in revenue-generating projects or activities; and (iii) Set aside fund
to cover up the cash deficiencies; and
b. Strong support of the Municipal Officials concerned for a continuing tight
control of all expenditures is enjoined.

7.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Install control over the issuance of EUF Tickets and AF 51 and its remittance
by designating an employee independent from Municipal Treasurer’s Office
that will randomly check its issuance and its remittance to the Liquidating
Officer; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) Stop issuing accountable forms to
erring accountable officers in order to avoid the practice of keeping
collections in their hands for a long period of time; and (ii) Refrain from
issuing EUF tickets to non-bonded employees pursuant to Section 101 of PD
1445, Section 305(f) of RA 7160, Section 50, Chapter 9, Subtitle B, Book V,
EO No 292 and Bureau of Treasury, Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated
August 5, 2009.

8.3. We recommended that the:
a. MDRRMO (i) prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the
format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to reflect the
allocation for QRF in lump sum and disaster mitigation, prevention and
preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded, as well as
the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of
previous years; (ii) ensure that all the proposed programs/projects for
disaster be implemented during the year to ascertain readiness of the
Municipality in case of calamity; and (iii) and in case there is change in the
previously proposed projects, see to it that the disaster council is guided by
DILG, DBM and National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Council Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 wherein the
unexpended balance of disaster fund may be released and utilized for new
expenditure items upon submission of a revised AIP as approved by the local
Sanggunian and the LCE upon recommendation the council and in
accordance with the approved disaster plan;
b. Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly the
Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified correct by the
Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month
in consonance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
c. BAC (i) explain the deficiencies noted above; and (ii) strictly adhere with the
pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement of
vehicles.
d. Municipal Accountant and BAC submit the supporting documents to
substantiate the transactions; and
e. Municipal Accountant (i) draw Journal Entry Voucher for the erroneous
recording of unexpended balance; and (ii) maintain subsidiary ledgers for
transfer of unutilized calamity fund to trust fund by year of transfer.

9.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and officials concerned:
a. Establish an SWM facility that can be used for a long-term period instead of
putting up a temporary one and eventually closing it due to non-compliance
or other issue and obtain an ECC to comply with the requirements R.A. No.
9003 and PD No. 1586 for the establishment of solid waste management
facility;
b. Strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the operation of a
dumpsite/sanitary landfill instead of the existing open dumpsite to mitigate
the harmful effects in the environment of solid wastes generated by the
locality and further damage to the health of its constituents;
c. Ensure that the constituents in the remaining 11 barangays illegally dumping
their waste elsewhere were informed of proper waste disposal. Moreover, we
recommended that the Designated MENRO ensure that all policies,
programs and activities regarding the solid waste management of the
Municipality were fully enforced; and
d. Call the attention of the junkshop owner in Barangay. Villaflor located just
along the national highway to properly file the wastes and immediately
dispose or recycle them.

10.4 We recommended that the MSWMB in assistance with the other department
head prioritize the revision of the SWMP in accordance with Section 17 of R.A No.
9003 for submission and approval by the NSWMC.

11.3 We recommended that the:
a. SB Members concerned provide explanation relative to their attendance in
the aforementioned events organized by the PCL and provide sufficient proof
to their actual attendance in the events;
b. Municipal Accountant (i) remind the employees concerned to liquidate their
CAs within the prescribed period provided in Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular
No. 97-002; (ii) closely monitor the liquidation of CAs; (iii) immediately issue
demand letter to officials and employees concerned with pending CAs; and
ensure that all claims must be supported with complete and valid
documentation, in case of the liquidation of CAs, scrutinize the Liquidation
Reports and see to it that it was supported with valid documents required by
COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the preparation of a particular Journal
Entry Voucher taking up the liquidation;
c. Municipal officials and employees concerned with CA for travel immediately
liquidate their CAs upon arrival from each travel and attach to the
liquidation report the copies of travel orders to ascertain the approved
travel;
d. Municipal Treasurer submit all the liquidation reports for the CAs granted
to officers and employees totaling ₱43,124.00 to ascertain the full liquidation
of cash advances; and
e. Accountable officers liquidate the remaining outstanding cash advances
within the prescribed regulatory period set by COA Circular No. 97-002.

12.3 We recommended that the Municipal Officials and Employees concerned
submit the necessary lacking documents for the payment of travelling expenses and
ensure the compliance with the provisions set forth in Section 4 of EO No. 298 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001.

13.6 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor and Officials observe strictly the austerity measures set
by DILG and minimize attendance to “Lakbay-Aral”, instead
appropriate funds to programs/projects/ activities that will bring
immediate socio-economic benefits among its local constituents;
b. Municipal Budget Officer explain the excess charging of the
expenses/disbursement against the appropriated amount for the
Employees’ Day and the Lakbay-Aral for the BHW, BNS among others;
c. Municipal Accountant submit the lacking documents to facilitate audit as
to legality and propriety of the disbursement, otherwise the transaction
would be considered irregular and refund of the full amount shall be
made in full immediately; and
d. BAC strictly adhere to the provision of Revised IRR of RA 9184 for the
procurement of services and explain the deficiencies noted above.

14.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman
explain the noted observation and submit all the lacking documents to avoid
suspension in audit.

15.3 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman
explain the noted observation and submit all the lacking documents to avoid
suspension in audit.

16.3 We recommended that the :
a. Municipality strictly adhere to the issuance of checks to financial
assistance clients in accordance with the provisions set in Section VI B.3.1
of DSWD AO. No. 5 series of 2011;
b. Municipal Accountant submit the necessary supporting documents;
c. Local Chief Executive formulate clear-cut policies and well-defined
guidelines for the granting of all types of donations/ financial assistance,
which shall provide among others, the following: (i) a clear definition of
the qualified beneficiaries and what kind of assistance or donation is
chargeable against a specific fund; (ii) the criteria in determining the
amount of donation to be given in every case and the designated person/s
that shall be responsible to determine the same; and (iii) complete list of
documents to be required for each type of donation/assistance. Moreover,
apportionment of funds to more beneficial programs and projects should
be carefully considered in order to achieve the desired betterment of the
whole constituency; and
d. Municipal Accountant submit the missing vouchers and their complete
supporting documents.

17.7 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer (i) provide appropriation for salaries of the
contractual personnel in the Municipality’s annual budget; (ii) strictly
apply AO No. 47 and Section 322 relative to the use of savings; and (iii)
ensure that funds shall be available exclusively for the specific purpose
for which they have been appropriated pursuant to Section 336 of RA
7160.

18.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit copy of documents
showing the written conformance from the Solicitor General or the Government
Corporate Counsel and written concurrence of the Commission on Audit to avoid
disallowance in audit.

19.10 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Waterworks System officials adopt austerity
measures by minimizing the variable operating cost of the PGWS, and
adopt a well-articulated policy for increased waterworks fee collection;
b. Municipal officials concerned consider the options in Section 3.4 of the
Local Budget Circular No. 100 wherein the personnel in the Operation of
PGWS be transferred to the Plantilla of Personnel under the General
Fund due to continuous incurrence of losses; and
c. Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate
financial statements for the LEE so that the Management can assess and
consider the viability of the Waterworks System being an economic
enterprise and for them to make well-informed decisions for its sustained
financial viability over the long term while delivering goods and services
to the general public.

15.3 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman
explain the noted observation and submit all the lacking documents to avoid
suspension in audit.

21.5 We recommended that the:
a. BAC Chairman explain the procurement of rice from supplier other than
NFA without securing waiver from the NFA;
b. Sanggunian Bayan member submit justification for rice amounting to
₱41,800.00; and
c. Henceforth, Management resort to NFA as primary supplier or source of rice
in all its procurements.

22.8 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health officer to maximize the
utilization of the PFPR as required under the Philhealth Circular No. 010
series of 2012 for the promotion of quality health care services; and
b. Municipal Accountant prepare the quarterly PFP Disposition and Allocation
Form and submit it to the Office of the Auditor for review in accordance
with Section XII of PhilHealth Circular No. 015 series of 2014.

23.10 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer’s Office employee in-charge with the preparation of
Report of Delinquent Market Stallholders provide the Municipal Accountant
with amount due and penalties for unpaid market fees to facilitate timely
recording of accrued rental income; and
b. Municipal Accountant adopt the accrual basis of recording the stall fees
pursuant to the Conceptual Framework for General Purpose Financial
Reporting and follow PPSAS No. 13.

24.10 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant exhaust all
the possible remedies provided under the existing rules and regulations to enforce
collections of receivables outstanding as of December 31, 2016.

24.11 If there are no possible remedies, the Municipal Mayor may file a request for
write-off supported with the documents as itemized in COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016 to the Commission on Audit.

25.7 We recommended that:
a. Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the monthly
monitoring Report of the Awarded Infra PPAs and List of Ongoing PPAs
and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and
b. Management pursue the timely submission of the reports within the
prescribed period to avoid the automatic suspension of the payment of
salary and other emoluments, and the imposition of administrative
disciplinary action upon the defaulting personnel concerned.

26.5 We recommended that the Management abide with the rules set forth under
Item 2.2.7 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to preserve public
interest.
